sheet_name: แปลนหลังคา
view_title: แปลนหลังคา
source_image: image/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02_หน้า08.png
grid_source: raw_json_ตัวที่ใช้งานจริง/04บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02/บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_02_หน้า00_gridline.json

element.grid_ref: covers the full plan grid1-3 x gridE-A plus overhangs — a single unbroken rectangle, NO notch/cutout (unlike house #3's roof plan which had a rectangular notch over an open carport)
element.description: หลังคาเหล็กรีดลอน (corrugated metal sheet roofing), hatched vertically over the whole roofed plan; two 'SLOPE 6°' labels printed vertically down the two roof faces (one each side of a central fold line running roughly north-south near grid2), indicating fall direction/pitch of approximately 6 degrees. Two curved swirl-arrow symbols near mid-plan, likely roof drainage/water-flow direction markers, not confidently classified further.
element.ridge_or_valley_lines: one internal fold line runs roughly north-south near grid2 (between the two 'SLOPE 6°' labels) — not confidently classified as a true ridge vs. valley at this resolution

element.grid_ref: dashed rectangle near grid2, close to the north edge (above gridE)
element.description: Dashed rectangular outline visible within the main roof hatch near the north edge — possibly a recess/step in the roof plane above the ห้องน้ำ3/WC3 canopy (กันสาด คสล +3.65 shown on the 2nd floor plan, หน้า07) projecting through the main roof, or a separate lower roof section. Not confidently classified.

element.grid_ref: dashed rectangle near grid2, close to the south edge (above gridA)
element.description: Dashed rectangular outline visible within the main roof hatch near the south edge — possibly related to the ระเบียง/กันสาด canopy below. Not confidently classified.

element.grid_ref: south-east corner, beyond gridA near grid2-3, level +6.65
element.description: 'กันสาด ค.ส.ล.' concrete canopy slab, floor-material box '5 +6.65 —' (blank opening ref) printed at the same location — this is a 3rd, roof-level canopy distinct from the two 2nd-floor-level canopies (+3.65/+3.75) already recorded on the floor plan (หน้า07).

warnings: This sheet (A-06) is the roof plan for the whole 2-story building — since the roof sits above the 2nd floor (+6.65 roof beam level, inferred from the same value as house #3's canopy level), its footprint corresponds to the 2nd-floor plan (หน้า07/A-05), not the ground floor.

Unlike house #3's roof plan (which had a rectangular notch over an open, roofless carport), this house's roof is a single unbroken rectangle over the full grid1-3 x gridE-A footprint plus overhangs — suggesting the ground-floor จอดรถ (carport, see _หน้า06_floor_plan_ground.json) is fully roofed here, not open to the sky. Not independently confirmed against a structural roof-framing sheet (outside this extraction's architecture-only scope).
The 'SLOPE 6°' labels are printed vertically along both roof faces, same convention and same 6-degree value as houses #2/#3's roof plans.

Overhang dimensions (1.30m east/west, 1.50m north/south) exactly match houses #2/#3's equivalent overhang figures despite this house having a different footprint size (8.50x10.00m vs house #3's), and are independently corroborated by the top/bottom dimension chains on the floor plan sheets (หน้า06/07) which show the same building-width/depth sub-splits.

Actual roof framing (purlins/rafters/trusses) is not detailed on this sheet — references to structural engineering drawings would be expected (outside this extraction's architecture-only page range 03-29), consistent with the section sheets to be read later (หน้า13-15).

Two dashed rectangular outlines near grid2 (north and south edges) and two curved swirl-arrow symbols near mid-plan are visible but not confidently classified at this resolution — flagged rather than guessed; may become clearer when cross-checked against the elevation/section sheets.
